title: Remove legacy artifacts from canonical flow and tooling
summary: Remove journal/snapshot/checkpoint from canonical resume path and tool exposure; keep handoff derived-only.
outputs: Canonical flow uses tx_event_log + tx_state only
Tool registry removes legacy artifact tools or marks them derived-only
RepoContext separates canonical vs derived artifact paths
acceptance_criteria: Resume logic ignores legacy journal/snapshot/checkpoint artifacts when canonical artifacts exist.
Tooling no longer exposes legacy artifact operations as canonical behavior.
Derived handoff is regenerated only after canonical state persistence.

steps.description: Audit resume/rebuild paths and remove reliance on legacy artifacts.
Update tool registry/router to avoid exposing legacy artifact tools as canonical.
Align repo context to treat tx_event_log/tx_state as canonical artifacts.

notes: Legacy files may remain on disk but must be ignored by canonical logic.
Handoff remains derived-only and regenerated from canonical state.
